# UC-22.2.51 · NIS2 Art.12/21 secure development and vulnerability disclosure evidence

## Description

Combines GitHub organisation audit events, open SonarQube blocker and critical findings, and CSV-tracked vulnerability-disclosure tickets so engineering gates and external reporter SLAs stay visible to compliance.

## Value

Product and legal leadership receive a single operational rhythm for secure build controls and coordinated disclosure rather than waiting for annual questionnaires to uncover stale scanner noise or unanswered researcher emails.

## Implementation

(1) Install Splunkbase 5598 and connect the GitHub App with least privilege. (2) Send SonarQube issues via HEC. (3) Maintain `vulnerability_disclosure_intake.csv` with legal. (4) Alert weekly; attach owners per `project_key`. (5) Archive to `index=audit_evidence`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Add-on for GitHub ([Splunkbase 5598](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5598)).
• Heavy forwarder with outbound `443/tcp` to `api.github.com` or your GHES hostname.
• SonarQube webhook to HEC token posting JSON with `sourcetype=sonarqube:issues`; validate with `curl` test event.
• Indexes `devops`, `sast`, `vuln`, `audit_evidence` provisioned.
• RBAC role `appsec_compliance` with read access only to required indexes.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Install the add-on on search head + heavy forwarder. In `local/inputs.conf` enable the audit modular input with `interval = 300` seconds and `index = devops`. Map `sourcetype=github:audit`. For SonarQube, sample `props.conf` should extract `severity`, `status`, `project_key`, `component`. Document API path `/api/issues/search` if polling instead of webhooks. Maintain `vulnerability_disclosure_intake.csv` with service owner column for routing.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Tighten `severity` list per risk appetite. Schedule twice weekly.

```spl
| inputlookup vulnerability_disclosure_intake.csv
| eval due_epoch=strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval intake_overdue=if(disclosure_status!="closed" AND now()>due_epoch,1,0)
| append [search index=sast sourcetype=sonarqube:issues severity IN ("BLOCKER","CRITICAL") status=OPEN earliest=-7d | stats count by project_key]
```

Append GitHub branch policy branch as shown in main `spl`. Wire lookup suppressions.

Step 3 — Validate
Pick repo with known protected branches; confirm `github:audit` events appear within 15 minutes. Run Sonar CLI scan to create a BLOCKER; ensure event hits Splunk within HEC flush window (default 30 seconds). Compare CSV row count to legal mailbox.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard with three panels: disclosures, Sonar roll-up, GitHub policy drift. PagerDuty low urgency for SAST, high for overdue disclosures past legal SLA. Monthly PDF to `index=audit_evidence` via scheduled report.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **403 from GitHub** — token missing `audit_log` scope; regenerate in org settings.
• **HEC 400** — JSON not indexed extractions; add `props.conf` `KV_MODE=json`.
• **Null branch audits** — enterprise only feature; remove GitHub append macro for teams not licensed.
• **Append result explosion** — add `| stats values(*) by disclosure_id` consolidation.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup vulnerability_disclosure_intake.csv
| eval due_epoch=strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval intake_overdue=if(disclosure_status!="closed" AND now()>due_epoch,1,0)
| where intake_overdue=1
| eval finding_type="disclosure_overdue", asset=coalesce(disclosure_id,reporter_email)
| fields finding_type, asset, disclosure_status, due_date
| append [
    search index=sast sourcetype=sonarqube:issues severity IN ("BLOCKER","CRITICAL") status=OPEN earliest=-7d
    | stats dc(key) as open_findings by project_key
    | eval finding_type="sast_open_critical", asset=project_key
]
| append [
    search index=devops sourcetype=github:audit earliest=-7d action="repo.access" OR action="protected_branch.*"
    | search NOT match(actor,"^(svc-|github-app)")
    | stats dc(repo) as repos_touched by actor
    | where repos_touched>15
    | eval finding_type="audit_volume_spike", asset=actor
]
| lookup nis2_secdv_kfp_exceptions asset OUTPUT exception_until
| where isnull(exception_until) OR now()>strptime(exception_until,"%Y-%m-%d")
| table finding_type, asset, disclosure_status, due_date, open_findings, repos_touched
```

## Visualization

Stacked bar of disclosure states, table of open critical SAST issues, timeline of `github:audit` branch events.
